---
title: "get_official_ranking"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `get_official_ranking()`

Create Official Ranking Table

## Usage

```r
get_official_ranking(
  token = "FLIX_PATROL",
  platform_name = "netflix",
  country_name,
  start_date,
  end_date,
  media_type,
  date_type = "week",
  language = "english"
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `token` | Character. API environment variable name. |
| `platform_name` | Character. Must be "netflix". |
| `country_name` | Character. Name of the country. |
| `start_date` | Date/Character. Range start (Monday). |
| `end_date` | Date/Character. Range end (Sunday). |
| `media_type` | Character. Content type. |
| `date_type` | Character. Defaults to "week". |
| `language_id` | Character. Language for lookup. |

## Value

A tibble of official rankings.

## Description

Fetches the official weekly rankings (currently Netflix exclusive).

## Details

Queries the '/rankingsofficial' endpoint. Note that as of the current
package version, FlixPatrol only provides official lists for Netflix.
This function enforces that restriction.
